channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump’s Palestinian refugee idea falls flat with Jordan and confounds a Senate ally
item.description: President Donald Trump’s push to have Egypt and Jordan take in large numbers of Palestinian refugees from besieged Gaza has fallen flat with the Amman government and perplexed a congressional ally.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Graham says Republicans may 'own another attack' on US if Trump border czar doesn't get deportation funds fast
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham, R-S.C., said congressional Republicans who delay funding for border czar Tom Homan could "own another attack on our country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump floats gutting the IRS, moving agents to the border armed with guns
item.description: President Donald Trump floated moving the thousands of IRS agents hired under the Biden administration to the southern border to help patrol the area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ramaswamy's expected run for Ohio governor would test experienced Republicans and tradition
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Fresh off of his unexpected departure from President Donald Trump's Department of Government Efficiency, Vivek Ramaswamy has set his sights on becoming governor of his home state.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH and THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ceasefire disputes between Israel and Hamas, Hezbollah throw region into turmoil
item.description: The ceasefire agreement between Israel, Hamas and Hezbollah is in new jeopardy Sunday as various groups slow walk responsibilities under the deal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California city delays vote to revoke sanctuary status
item.description: The mayor of El Cajon, California, wants to revoke the town's sanctuary city status but faced backlash from residents.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Zouves, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's border emergency declaration comes amid relative calm after years of major turmoil
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Long stretches of silence on a Border Patrol scanner are punctuated with updates on tracking a single migrant for hours. The radio traffic sounds like a throwback to earlier times, before the United States became the largest destination for asylum-seekers in 2017.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, GREGORY BULL and EUGENE GARCIA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida Republican urges Trump to spare some migrants under deportation plan
item.description: Salazar, a daughter of Cuban exiles, represents Florida's 27th Congressional District which is home to a number of Venezuelans and Cubans.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: The CIA believes COVID most likely originated from a lab but has low confidence in its own finding
item.description: The CIA now believes the virus responsible for the COVID-19 pandemic most likely originated from a laboratory.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Nikki Fried vows to rebuild Florida Democrats as re-elected chair
item.description: Nikki Fried was reelected Saturday as chair of the Florida Democratic Party, which is seeking to regain relevance in a state that has tilted decidedly toward the Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Democrats and voting groups say a bid to toss out North Carolina ballots is an attack on democracy
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — Copland Rudolph cast a ballot in the November election, just as she has for years, with her vote counting on a long list of North Carolina contests that were settled soon after.
item.dc:creator: BY GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate puts Noem on doorstep of DHS confirmation
item.description: South Dakota Gov. Kristi Noem (R) moved a step closer to leading the Department of Homeland Security as the Senate voted on Friday to advance her to a final confirmation vote. Senators voted 61-39 to break a filibuster on Noem’s nomination on Friday evening.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Conservatives of color have lofty expectations for Trump's second term
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Delivering his first address as a reinaugurated president, Donald Trump spoke directly to communities that had historically shunned his party.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: State Department freezes new funding for nearly all US aid programs worldwide
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The State Department ordered a sweeping freeze Friday on new funding for almost all U.S. foreign assistance, making exceptions for emergency food programs and military aid to Israel and Egypt. The order threatened a quick halt to many of the billions of dollars in U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida schools will comply with ICE on campuses: Education department
item.description: Schools across America, including in Florida, are gearing up for the possibility of officials with Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) showing up to their campuses.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump leans in on targeting Russian oil revenue as he tries to fulfill pledge to end Ukraine war
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is emphasizing that targeting Russia's oil revenue is the best way to get Moscow to end its nearly three-year war against Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Defense Department orders 'pause' of social media accounts
item.description: The Defense Department said it needs time to review its social media programming to match Trump's priorities.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Measure to make California an independent country cleared to gather signatures
item.description: Another ballot measure asking whether California should be its own country is cleared to start gathering signatures, the California Secretary of State announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he wants to overhaul FEMA as he travels to North Carolina and California
item.description: President Trump is taking the first trip of his term on Friday to North Carolina and California, visiting communities grappling with recovery from natural disasters.
item.dc:creator: Asma Khalid and Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: Trump's Cabinet picks are set for Senate hearings. Here's the schedule
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nominations process for President Donald Trump's appointees is picking up pace, with some confirmed by the Senate and hearings scheduled for more of his Cabinet picks. Many nominees have met with senators individually.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota justices tell lawmakers to work out control of ‘dysfunctional’ House
item.description: The Minnesota Supreme Court on Friday agreed with DFLers that it takes 68 votes to conduct House business, but the opinion didn’t come with a road map on how the House should proceed from here.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Trump pulls security for Pompeo, others amid Iran threats
item.description: President Trump pulled security protection for former Secretary of State Mike Pompeo and his top aide, Brian Hook, both of whom have faced threats from Iran amid their hard-line positions toward the Islamic Republic, multiple outlets reported on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Walz proposes $887 million bonding bill for infrastructure projects, not worried about Capitol impasse
item.description: Using the breached Rapidan Dam as a backdrop Gov. Tim Walz pitched his $887 million bonding bill. As well as funding for infrastructure, Walz wants to fund a new state patrol headquarters, and address housing and environmental needs.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Top Minnesota attorney: Birthright citizenship suit against Trump just the beginning
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily blocked President Donald Trump’s executive order redefining birthright citizenship two days after Minnesota joined 21 other states in a lawsuit to challenge it. Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison discussed his strategy.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Megan Burks, MPR News

item.title: Senate confirms John Ratcliffe as Trump's CIA director
item.description: The Senate on Thursday confirmed John Ratcliffe to lead the Central Intelligence Agency in overwhelming bipartisan fashion, making him the second member of President Trump’s national security team to be approved by the upper chamber. Senators confirmed Ratcliffe in a 74-25 vote.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: Stick with us: Why the internet is obsessed with one of the simplest tools known to man
item.description: "Stick Nation" is a worldwide movement that thrives on irreverent, fantastical and downright wholesome descriptions of sticks.
item.dc:creator: Alina Hartounian, MPR News

item.title: How Trump's gender order impacts Californians' passports, driver's licenses
item.description: President Donald Trump's executive order declaring the U.S. only recognizes two genders, male and female, has raised questions about what this means for the validity of documents distributed to transgender Californians with their preferred gender marker.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: House Republicans seek to roll back DFL taxes, fees tied to transportation programs
item.description: Minnesota House Republicans are seeking to roll back taxes and fees approved when Democrats had full control of state government. A delivery fee and automatic increases to the gas taxes are on the GOP chopping block.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: GOP congressman wants bishop 'added to deportation list' after Trump prayer service
item.description: Rep. Mike Collins heavily criticized statements made by the Right Rev. Mariann Budde on Tuesday at the inaugural prayer service held for President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Trump popularity higher than throughout most of first term: Poll
item.description: The poll found Trump with a 47 percent approval rating, higher than almost the entirety of his first term, though still below the majority support that most other presidents have enjoyed at the start of their administrations.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WFLA, NBC

item.title: First female Coast Guard commandant ousted
item.description: Coast Guard Commandant Adm. Linda Fagan, the first female uniformed leader of an Armed Forces branch in U.S. history, was ousted by the Trump administration with no explanation late Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Gen. Milley portrait taken down from Pentagon
item.description: The portrait, which had just been unveiled Jan. 10, hung in the Joint Chiefs hallway next to those of other former chairs.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, Fox 5

item.title: Why Biden pardoned Mark Milley ahead of Trump taking office
item.description: Former President Joe Biden's pre-emptive pardon for retired Gen. Mark Milley, the former chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, will give the retired military official a shield against any action that President Donald Trump might take against him amid their highly public feud.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, Fox 5

item.title: Here are the biggest fights Democrats are bracing for in Trump's second term
item.description: House Democrats are bracing for major fights in the coming year as President Trump enters the White House for his second term and Republicans control all levers of power in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, Fox 5

item.title: Florida officials react to Trump inauguration
item.description: Donald Trump was sworn in as the 47th President of the United States on Monday. Here's how Florida officials are responding.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump sworn in as 47th president, celebrates with supporters
item.description: On a frigid holiday Monday in Washington, D.C., Donald Trump took the oath of office for a second time to become the 47th president of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, PIX 11

item.title: Trump to issue executive orders defining sexes, ending DEI practices in government
item.description: President-elect Trump intends to issue executive orders on his first day in office that will recognize only two sexes, male and female, and aim to end diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) practices in the federal government, according to incoming White House officials.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5
